title: Eng Review
use_when: Use Eng Review when an architecture plan, implementation slice, data flow, integration, migration, technical risk, or test strategy needs engineering judgment before build, QA, release, docs, or DX work proceeds.
do_not_use_when: Do not use Eng Review for product thesis, requirements priority, UX polish, QA execution, release coordination, documentation wording, DX usability, or stakeholder messaging unless the issue is technical execution risk.
output_artifact: Engineering review with architecture readout, execution risks, test strategy, decision, and handoff
framework_fit: Eng Review fits the technical gate inside 0-1, operate, and grow when system boundaries, data flow, performance, security, observability, and test strategy must be credible before the product moves forward.
failure_modes: Approves a plan without tracing the actual data flow, state transitions, dependencies, failure modes, and verification coverage.
Turns into product or design critique instead of focusing on technical execution risks that can block build, QA, release, docs, or DX readiness.
Treats tests as an afterthought rather than specifying the unit, integration, eval, and verification evidence needed for the next gate.

examples.prompt: Use $eng-review to review this architecture and implementation plan for data flow, risk, test strategy, and build readiness.
examples.expected_artifact: Engineering review with architecture readout, execution risks, test strategy, decision, and handoff
